unexpected_finds.title: 5%-threshold OPT_X4-trigger heuristic (synth-supplied; not previously codified)
unexpected_finds.detail: Q-194-2 step 2 (serial decompression) returning os error 5 on >5% of .ltar paths is synth's proposed ladder-fall-through threshold. This is a tactical decision criterion the operator/CLI did not previously have a number for. The 5% figure is heuristic (no math behind it) but acts as a defensible cut-off to avoid sunk-cost continuation if serial-mode also fails. Worth codifying.
unexpected_finds.memory_text_proposed: Windows Lean Mathlib cache decompression OPT_X4-trigger threshold: if serial leantar decompression (one .ltar at a time) still hits os error 5 on >5% of paths, host-environment problem confirmed; switch to OPT_X4 (defer Lean axis) rather than continue ops debug.

unexpected_finds.title: Cascade ledger discipline rule: deferred-axis RULE-1-lift-gate tagging
unexpected_finds.detail: Q-194-4 verdict states (verbatim): 'when OPT_X4 is selected, M10-V0-lean-sorry-discharge must be tagged as a RULE-1 lift gate in the cascade ledger so the deferral is visible and the eventual lift attempt cannot proceed until M10 is reopened and closed. Provided that tagging is recorded at the bridge where the deferral happens, OPT_X4 is acceptable.' This generalizes to a SIARC discipline rule: whenever an axis is deferred under a RULE-N pause, the deferred axis's closing item must be tagged at the deferral bridge as a RULE-N lift gate. Codifies a previously implicit pattern.
unexpected_finds.memory_text_proposed: When an axis is deferred under an active RULE-N pause, the deferred axis's closing artefact MUST be tagged in the cascade ledger at the deferral bridge as a 'RULE-N lift gate'. The eventual lift attempt cannot proceed cleanly without explicit gate-tagging-recorded discipline.

unexpected_finds.title: Synth identifies Windows os error 5 signature as concurrent file-handle contention
unexpected_finds.detail: Q-194-1 substantiation diagnoses the 7801/8399 failure pattern as 'the signature of concurrent file-handle contention, not a single permission bit' and enumerates 3 contenders: OneDrive virtualization, Defender real-time scan, VS Code Lean server .olean/.ilean locks. This concurrent-contention hypothesis is testable: serial decompression (ladder step 2) should converge if hypothesis correct. Worth flagging for the next memory promotion if confirmed.

unexpected_finds.title: Parallel-track ordering (c) green-lights immediate FIRE-A2' Phase 0 fire
unexpected_finds.detail: Q-194-3 verdict explicitly endorses (c) parallel ordering: operator runs OPT_X2 in background while CLI fires FIRE-A2' Phase 0 substrate-prep in foreground. No resource contention (different process trees, different filesystem paths). This unlocks the FIRE-A2' Phase 0 cascade NOW without waiting for Lean-axis resolution, advancing the only remaining non-Lean math axis toward FIRE-A4' deliverable.
